metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete `project.config` stanza for the Rebase access right (e.g. `label Rebase = group RebarBotNava`) so the executor can write it directly; if the exact form is version-specific, pin it to the confirmed Gerrit version in use and include the verified snippet in the plan.
findings.finding: The Rebase AC explicitly defers the exact access-right name and config form to implementation-time investigation ('Verify at implementation the exact access-right name/form... against this instance's access-control.html... then encode it in project.config'). An executing agent cannot write the project.config entry or setup-project.sh grant without first stopping to resolve this Gerrit-version-specific question — a real placeholder deferring a design decision to the executor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' bullet
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a verified project.config snippet to the plan body, e.g.:
```
[access "refs/heads/*"]
  rebase = group RebarBotNava
```
and note the Gerrit version it was confirmed against (check `access-control.html` once, record the result here rather than deferring to the executor).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite the Rebase AC as a specific observable end-state, e.g. 'project.config contains `rebase = group <BotGroup>` under `[access "refs/heads/*"]` and a live API call with `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` returns HTTP 200 with the uploader field matching the original patch-set uploader.'
findings.finding: The Rebase AC is partially effort-framed: it instructs the executor to 'verify... then encode it in project.config' rather than stating the specific observable outcome (what the project.config entry looks like when correct). This makes the criterion's pass/fail state dependent on executor judgment rather than a checkable artifact.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve and pin the exact Gerrit 3.14.1 `project.config` stanza for the `Rebase` access right that enables `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` (e.g. by checking `access-control.html` once now and recording the verified snippet in the plan body), so the executor can write it directly without a second investigation.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the Gerrit 'Rebase' access right on this instance (Gerrit 3.14.1) enables rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader via the REST API — citing 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html' — but then immediately defers the exact access-right name and project.config stanza to implementation-time verification. No captured doc artifact, URL, or concrete project.config snippet is present in the plan or repo. This is an unresolved capability assertion: the central deliverable cannot be authored without first resolving what the plan explicitly leaves open.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' bullet; Files: infra/gerrit/project.config + infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh
findings.suggested_fix:
Consult `https://<gerrit-host>/Documentation/access-control.html` on the live 3.14.1 instance, confirm the exact permission token name (likely `rebase` in lowercase, not `label-Rebase`), then add the verified snippet to the plan body under the Rebase AC:
```
[access "refs/heads/*"]
  rebase = group Contributors   # or a dedicated bot group
```
and note: 'Verified against Gerrit 3.14.1 access-control.html on <date>'. This eliminates the implementation-time ambiguity and ensures the project.config push succeeds with the right semantics.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which group (existing or new) is granted the `Rebase` access right in `project.config`, update the `want=` dict in `setup-project.sh` accordingly, and describe any new group creation step.
findings.finding: The plan mandates a `Rebase` access right in `project.config` on `refs/heads/*` but never specifies which GROUP is granted the right. The `setup-project.sh` `want=` dict (line 123) maps exactly four groups; no new group for Rebase holders is named, nor is an existing group (e.g. `Service Users` or `Contributors`) designated. Without the group name the `project.config` stanza and `groups` file cannot be written, and the `want=` dict cannot be updated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123 / infra/gerrit/project.config [access "refs/heads/*"]
findings.suggested_fix: Either reuse `Service Users` (since `RebarBotNava` is provisioned there via `service-user.sh`) and add `rebase = group Service Users` to `[access "refs/heads/*"]`, or create a new `Autolanders` group, name it in the plan, add it to `setup-project.sh`'s group-creation block and `want=` dict, and add `rebase = group Autolanders` to `project.config`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback decision rule for the Rebase access-right verification step: specify what to do if the right is absent or differently named in this Gerrit version (e.g., alternative ACL, file a follow-on ticket, use a different group permission).
findings.finding: The plan explicitly defers the exact `project.config` access-right token for on-behalf-of-uploader rebase to 'verify at implementation against this instance's access-control.html' but specifies no fallback protocol if the `Rebase` right is absent, renamed, or works differently in this Gerrit version. This is an unspecified fallback for a known-uncertain sub-step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)': 'Verify at implementation the exact access-right name/form … then encode it in project.config'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `autolander-gerrit-token` should use `get_param` (required, fail-fast) or `get_param_optional` (soft) in `fetch-secrets.sh`, and document the rationale (i.e. whether the autolander token must be populated before any container can start).
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `fetch-secrets.sh` should fetch `autolander-gerrit-token` into `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` but does not specify whether to use `get_param` (required/fail-fast) or `get_param_optional` (soft/non-blocking). The existing script makes this distinction explicit for every token; the wrong choice has direct reliability consequences (a missing token silently produces a blank env-var vs. aborting boot).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Credential' bullet: 'fetched by fetch-secrets.sh into the shared .env as AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN' / infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Use `get_param_optional` with a downstream fail-loud guard in the autolander container's entrypoint (similar to the OAUTH pattern), so the review-bot container can still start if the autolander token is not yet provisioned — decoupling deployment ordering.

findings.finding: AC3's 'bot can authenticate + submit a test change on the instance' does not specify what test change is used (pre-existing change ID, synthetically created change, specific repo), making the smoke-test non-deterministic and potentially environment-destructive if the wrong change is submitted.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet: 'the bot can authenticate + submit a test change on the instance'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan whether `autolander-gerrit-token` uses `get_param` (required, fail-fast) or `get_param_optional` in `fetch-secrets.sh`, and justify the choice given shared-instance boot coupling vs. silent-failure risk.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether `autolander-gerrit-token` should be fetched as a required (`get_param`, fail-fast) or optional (`get_param_optional`) secret in `fetch-secrets.sh`. Using `get_param` (required) couples every boot of the single shared instance to the token being pre-populated — even before the autolander container is deployed — which could block instance bring-up. Using `get_param_optional` silently starts the autolander container with a blank token. The plan's AC says 'fetched by `fetch-secrets.sh` into the shared `.env` as `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`' but does not resolve required-vs-optional.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Credential bullet; infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Use `get_param_optional` in `fetch-secrets.sh` (matching `reviewbot-tickets-pat`), and add an explicit CHANGEME/blank guard inside the autolander container startup (e.g., entrypoint exits non-zero if `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` is empty), so the failure is loud at the service level rather than the boot level.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before encoding `rebase = group <X>` in project.config, confirm the exact config-file token for the Rebase access right against this instance's `/a/access/rebar` REST response or `/access-control.html` on Gerrit 3.14.1, and record the verified token in the plan.
findings.finding: The `Rebase` access-right config-file token for project.config is asserted to exist on this Gerrit 3.14.x instance but is unverified — no `rebase =` line appears anywhere in infra/gerrit/project.config or setup-project.sh, and the plan itself defers confirmation to implementation time ('Verify at implementation the exact access-right name/form … against this instance's access-control.html'). If the ACL token differs from what the plan expects (e.g., it requires a capability-level grant or a plugin-specific name), the project.config push will either be silently ignored or rejected, leaving the bot unable to rebase on behalf of the uploader.
findings.location: AC: 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' + Files: infra/gerrit/project.config
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit pre-implementation step: `curl -u admin:<token> https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/a/access/rebar | python3 -m json.tool | grep -i rebase` — if the key appears in the response, record it; if absent, probe All-Projects. Encode the confirmed token in project.config and setup-project.sh before merging the plan.

findings.finding: The plan does not declare whether AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN should be fetched as *required* (fail-fast, like gerrit-bot-token) or *optional* (silent blank, like reviewbot-tickets-pat) in fetch-secrets.sh. If treated as optional, the autolander container will start with an empty credential and every Gerrit API call will 401 silently — an undeclared access posture on the container→Gerrit boundary.
findings.location: AC — Credential bullet / infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh edit
findings.suggested_fix: In the Files / fetch-secrets.sh section, add: 'Use get_param (required, fail-fast), matching the treatment of gerrit-bot-token — the autolander cannot function without this credential so a CHANGEME or absent value must abort boot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC: (1) `grep AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN .env` to prove credential materialization; (2) a `gerrit` REST/SSH call to confirm `RebarBotNava ∈ Contributors`; (3) an explicit `curl` to `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` and an assertion that the resulting change owner equals the original uploader; (4) run `setup-project.sh` twice and `diff` the resulting `project.config` to assert idempotency.
findings.finding: No concrete proving commands are given for any AC: credential materialization, group-membership verification, rebase-on-behalf API call, idempotency re-run, or submit. The smoke-check AC describes outcomes in prose ('confirms ... uploader preserved') without a single executable command, leaving done-ness unverifiable without author judgment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all four bullets, especially 'Tests' bullet

findings.finding: None of the three acceptance criteria include a concrete proving command. AC1 (SSM/env) has no aws-cli or grep command; AC2 (capability grant) has no Gerrit SSH/REST query; AC3 (smoke) uses 'confirms' without specifying the command, target URL, or expected HTTP status — leaving done-ness unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet; all three AC checkboxes

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan: (a) specify autolander service startup behavior when `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` is absent or equals the CHANGEME sentinel (fail-fast with a clear log, not a silent auth-retry loop); (b) add a RETIRE step (remove the SSM parameter from the `for_each` set, remove the `fetch-secrets.sh` mapping, remove the Gerrit Rebase ACL) to the plan's lifecycle documentation.
findings.finding: The shared `.env` is consumed by at least two bots (review bot via `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN`, autolander via `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`), but the plan does not specify consumer tolerance for an absent or stale `.env` (e.g., on first deploy before the secret is rotated from CHANGEME, or after a failed fetch-secrets run).
findings.location: infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh + AC Credential bullet — shared `.env` lifecycle

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify which group(s) the Rebase ACL will be granted to and verify they are (or will be added to) the `want` dict in `setup-project.sh` line 123; if a new group is needed, add both the `create-group` step and the `want` entry to the plan's Files/AC section.
findings.finding: The plan adds a `Rebase` ACL stanza to `project.config` (granting it to at least one group, e.g., 'Service Users'), but the `want` associative array in `setup-project.sh` (line 123) is hardcoded to exactly {Administrators, Service Users, feature-branch-drivers, Contributors}. Any group referenced in the new Rebase ACL that is NOT in `want` will cause `setup-project.sh` to omit its UUID from the `groups` file, and Gerrit will reject the `refs/meta/config` push with an 'unknown group' error. The plan never mentions updating the `want` dict.
findings.location: infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123 / AC: Rebase-on-behalf access right
findings.suggested_fix: In the Files section for `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`, explicitly state: (a) which group holds the Rebase right (e.g., 'Service Users' — already in `want`), and (b) if a new group is created, add it to the `want` dict and include a group-create + idempotent-membership convergence block mirroring the Contributors pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which group receives the `Rebase` ACL grant in `project.config`, and confirm (or add) that group name to the `want` dict in `setup-project.sh` so UUID resolution succeeds.
findings.finding: setup-project.sh's `want` dict (line 123) hardcodes the group names that need UUID resolution for the `groups` file. The plan says to add a `Rebase` ACL in project.config but never specifies which group receives it — if the chosen group (e.g., `Service Users`, `Autolanders`, or any name not already in `want`) is not in that dict, the refs/meta/config push will fail with an 'unknown group' error. The `want` dict update is an unenumerated side-effect of the project.config edit.
findings.location: infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123 / Files section: setup-project.sh EDIT
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan note: 'Grant Rebase to [group name, e.g., Contributors or Service Users]; if a new group is created, add it to setup-project.sh's want dict and the group-creation block before the refs/meta/config push.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the group that will hold the `Rebase` right in the plan, and add an explicit note that `setup-project.sh`'s `want` dict and (if needed) the `groups` UUID-resolution block must be updated to include that group before the `refs/meta/config` push.
findings.finding: The plan adds a `Rebase` access right to `project.config` but defers the exact group to 'Verify at implementation', without noting that `setup-project.sh`'s `want` dict (line 123) must be updated to include any newly-referenced group. If the implementer grants `Rebase` to a group not already in `want` (e.g., a new `Autolander` group or `Service Users`), the `refs/meta/config` push will be rejected by Gerrit with an 'unknown group' error — silently breaking idempotent re-runs. `Service Users` uses Gerrit's internal UUID and is not resolved via `ls-groups`, so it also needs special handling if chosen.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-on-behalf access right bullet; infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123; infra/gerrit/project.config
findings.suggested_fix: Grant `rebase` to the existing `Service Users` group (the bot is already a Service User), document its well-known UUID handling (like `global:Registered-Users`), and add a `global:Service-Users	Service Users` line to the groups file block in setup-project.sh — no new group required, no `want` dict change needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan which group receives the `rebase` ACL entry in project.config, and confirm that group is either already in setup-project.sh's `want` dict or that the dict will be updated (and the group created/converged) as part of this story.
findings.finding: If the `Rebase` ACL in project.config is granted to a new group (rather than an existing one like `Contributors` or `Service Users`), setup-project.sh's hard-coded `want` dict (`declare -A want=( ["Administrators"]=1 ["Service Users"]=1 ["feature-branch-drivers"]=1 ["Contributors"]=1 )`) will omit that group's UUID from the `groups` file, causing Gerrit to reject the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error. The plan does not specify which group receives the Rebase right, leaving this as an unresolved gap.
findings.location: infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123 + Files section: 'infra/gerrit/project.config + infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh'
findings.suggested_fix: If `Contributors` is the intended grantee (the bot is already a member), add `rebase = group Contributors` to project.config's `[access "refs/heads/*"]` block — no `want` dict change needed. Make this explicit in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the parent ticket `perpetual-ultrasonic-raccoon` `file_impact` entry for `setup-project.sh` to match S5b's AC: replace 'All-Projects [capability] rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader' with 'rebar project refs/meta/config per-ref Rebase ACL on refs/heads/*'.
findings.finding: The parent ticket's `file_impact` entry for `setup-project.sh` describes the mechanism as 'All-Projects [capability] rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader + submit on refs/heads/*' — directly contradicting S5b's AC which explicitly rules out both All-Projects scope and [capability] blocks. An implementer loading the parent ticket gets a conflicting instruction for the same file.
findings.location: Parent ticket `perpetual-ultrasonic-raccoon` file_impact for `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` vs. S5b AC 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the plan (and/or add a clarifying note at the top) to remove 'All-Projects capability' since the plan explicitly places the Rebase right on the rebar project only and forbids a global capability grant — the title contradicts the body.
findings.finding: The plan title declares 'All-Projects capability' but the body and AC explicitly state the Rebase right is NOT All-Projects and NOT a global [capability] — the title names a concept that is contradicted throughout the plan body, which would send an implementer searching for All-Projects work that doesn't exist.
findings.location: Plan title vs. Files section ('NOT All-Projects') and AC 'Rebase-on-behalf access right' ('NOT a global [capability]')
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to e.g. 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase ACL provisioning' and drop 'All-Projects capability' from the title entirely, since the plan explicitly rules out All-Projects and global-capability grants.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the plan to match what it actually delivers, e.g. 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + per-project Rebase access right (no new submit ACL)' — removing the false promises of 'All-Projects capability' and 'submit ACL provisioning'.
findings.finding: The plan title promises 'All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning' but the body explicitly delivers neither: it rejects a global All-Projects capability in favour of a per-ref Rebase right, and it explicitly rejects adding a submit ACL because one already exists. An implementer reading only the title would build the wrong thing.
findings.location: Plan title: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning'
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase-on-behalf access right for rebar project' and add a brief note in the Why section: 'No All-Projects capability or new submit ACL is introduced; the Rebase access right is per-project and submit is already covered by existing group membership.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase` exists and accepts `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` (exact field name) on this Gerrit 3.14.1 instance before finalizing the plan, and confirm whether `/rebase:chain` is available; record results as MATCH/MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED in the plan.
findings.finding: The `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` body field on `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase` (and the `/rebase:chain` endpoint) is a new REST surface not previously used by any script in this repo. Its existence on this specific Gerrit 3.14.x instance and its exact field name are unverified — no existing script calls either endpoint.
findings.location: AC: 'to call `rebase`/`rebase:chain` with `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the Gerrit ACL design rationale (Rebase right vs. submit-strategy, exclusiveGroupPermissions eclipsing, no All-Projects grant) in a persistent artifact — an ADR, a comment block in project.config/setup-project.sh, or a CLAUDE.md section — so future maintainers understand why the ACL is structured this way.
findings.finding: The plan encodes nuanced, Gerrit-version-specific reasoning (why Rebase ACL vs. submit-strategy, why not All-Projects, why exclusiveGroupPermissions blocks an inherited submit grant) inline in the ticket but names no persistent artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md, design doc) to capture it — leaving future maintainers without a durable record of why the ACL is structured this way.
findings.location: AC: Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism); Files: infra/gerrit/project.config + setup-project.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prominent comment block in `infra/gerrit/project.config` and `setup-project.sh` explaining: (1) why `Rebase` ACL (not submit-strategy) is needed for rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader, (2) why no All-Projects submit grant (eclipsed by exclusiveGroupPermissions), and (3) that the exact right name should be re-verified against access-control.html on Gerrit upgrade. Alternatively, create a short ADR in `docs/adr/` referencing this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `fetch-secrets.sh` writes to a temp file then atomically replaces the `.env` (write-tempfile + mv), aborts with a non-zero exit and a logged error on any SSM fetch failure, and that the calling process treats a non-zero exit as a fatal deployment error rather than continuing with a stale/partial `.env`.
findings.finding: `fetch-secrets.sh` edits are specified with no error handling: if the AWS SSM call for the new `autolander-gerrit-token` fails mid-script, the shared `.env` may be left partially written, silently removing or truncating the existing `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN` — breaking the review bot with no observable signal.
findings.location: infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh — AC: 'fetched by fetch-secrets.sh into the shared .env as AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN'
findings.suggested_fix: Pattern: write all fetched values to `${ENV_FILE}.tmp`, then `mv ${ENV_FILE}.tmp ${ENV_FILE}` only if all fetches succeeded; `set -euo pipefail` at the top of the script ensures any failed AWS CLI call aborts before the mv.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update the `ssm.tf` comment to reflect that some params are fetch-secrets.sh-only (not user_data.sh), or note in the plan that this comment divergence is intentional and acceptable.
findings.finding: The plan adds `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` to `rebar_secret_params` in `ssm.tf`, but `ssm.tf` line 16 contains an explicit invariant comment: 'keep in sync with user_data.sh and ADR 0012'. The autolander token is intentionally NOT added to `user_data.sh`'s PARAMS array (which writes `/etc/rebar/.env`) — this deliberately breaks the stated sync invariant. The plan does not acknowledge this and does not call for updating the comment or the `user_data.sh` PARAMS note.
findings.location: infra/terraform/ssm.tf line 16 / infra/terraform/user_data.sh lines 88-102
findings.suggested_fix: Update ssm.tf line 16 comment to: 'EXACT secret parameter names — params marked (fetch-secrets only) are not in user_data.sh/ADR 0012 (container-only creds).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare per-service env-var scoping in the docker-compose service definitions (or a companion S5a spec) so `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` is injected only into the autolander service, `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN` only into the review-bot, and `GERRIT_ADMIN_PASSWORD` only into services that genuinely need it — rather than all credentials being available to all containers via the shared `.env`.
findings.finding: The plan adds `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` (a credential with Submit + Rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader rights) to the shared `.env` consumed by all compose services, but does not declare per-service env-var scoping in docker-compose. If services mount `.env` wholesale (e.g., `env_file: .env`), the autolander's submit-capable token is visible to the review-bot container and any other service — and the review-bot's token plus `GERRIT_ADMIN_PASSWORD` are visible to the autolander — widening the blast radius of a single-container compromise.
findings.location: infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh — shared `.env` output / AC: Credential bullet
findings.suggested_fix: In the autolander's compose service definition, use `environment:` to enumerate only the vars it needs (`AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`, etc.) rather than `env_file: .env`. Alternatively, split secrets into per-service SSM paths fetched at container startup (each service fetches its own subset), or use Docker secrets / tmpfs injection scoped per service.

findings.finding: `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` is asserted to be a grantable server-level `[capability]` in `All-Projects refs/meta/config`, but this is an unverified capability assertion: in Gerrit 3.14.1 the documented global capabilities (administrateServer, runAs, streamEvents, etc.) do not include `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader`. It is a project-level submit strategy setting (`[submit] rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader = true` in project.config), not a per-user capability grant — if the plan's mechanism is wrong, `setup-all-projects.sh` would silently no-op or be rejected by Gerrit, leaving the bot unable to rebase on behalf of uploaders.
findings.location: AC: Capability — '`rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` server-level capability (`All-Projects` `refs/meta/config` `[capability]`)' + Files: `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh` (NEW)
findings.suggested_fix: In `infra/gerrit/project.config` under `[submit]`, add `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader = true` (alongside the existing `action = fast forward only` or switching to `action = rebase if necessary` as appropriate for the auto-lander flow). Drop the `[capability]` grant from `setup-all-projects.sh`'s scope, since this is a per-project submit-strategy config, not a per-user server capability.

findings.finding: The plan's AC instructs `setup-all-projects.sh` to grant `submit` on `refs/heads/*` via the All-Projects `refs/meta/config`, but the existing `infra/gerrit/project.config` already declares `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` on `refs/heads/*`. Under Gerrit semantics, `exclusiveGroupPermissions` causes the project to IGNORE any inherited All-Projects grant for matching refs — so an All-Projects submit grant for `RebarBotNava` would be silently eclipsed and have no effect for the `rebar` project. The submit permission must be added to the `rebar` project's own `refs/meta/config` (managed by `setup-project.sh` / `infra/gerrit/project.config`), not to All-Projects.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — Capability bullet: 'plus submit permission on refs/heads/*'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `RebarBotNava` to the `Contributors` group (already the default in `setup-project.sh` via `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS`) or add an explicit `submit = group Service Users` line under `[access "refs/heads/*"]` in `infra/gerrit/project.config`, and remove the submit-grant duty from `setup-all-projects.sh` scope (leaving that script to handle only the server-level `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` capability). Update `setup-all-projects.sh` Files entry accordingly.

findings.finding: The plan's AC claims the bot needs a `submit` grant via `setup-all-projects.sh` on All-Projects `refs/heads/*` to avoid 403s on `POST /submit`, but this mechanism cannot work for the `rebar` project: `project.config` already sets `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` on `refs/heads/*`, which causes Gerrit to IGNORE any inherited All-Projects submit grant for that ref pattern. An All-Projects submit ACL is therefore a no-op for `rebar`. The real coverage comes from `RebarBotNava` being in the `Contributors` group (already the default in `setup-project.sh`: `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS="${CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS:-RebarBotNava}"`). The plan should either (a) drop the All-Projects submit grant (it does nothing for `rebar`) and rely on the existing Contributors membership, or (b) clarify that `setup-all-projects.sh` grants submit only on All-Projects as a belt-and-suspenders for other projects — but note this doesn't fix the `rebar` 403 path, which is already covered.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Capability bullet: 'plus submit permission on refs/heads/* (project.config restricts submit to Contributors+Administrators, so the bot must hold it or every POST /submit 403s)'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the 'plus submit permission on refs/heads/*' clause from the `setup-all-projects.sh` scope (the bot already has submit via Contributors in the rebar project, and All-Projects submit is overridden by exclusiveGroupPermissions). The script's sole Gerrit-ACL work is the `[capability]` rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader grant. Add a comment noting that submit is covered by Contributors membership in setup-project.sh.

findings.finding: The plan proposes granting `submit` on `refs/heads/*` in `All-Projects` for `RebarBotNava` via `setup-all-projects.sh`, but `rebar`'s existing `project.config` already has `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` which causes Gerrit to IGNORE the inherited All-Projects submit grant for `rebar` — making the All-Projects grant a no-op for this project. Additionally, `RebarBotNava` is already added to the `Contributors` group by `setup-project.sh` (default `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS`), which already holds `submit` on `refs/heads/*` in the rebar project. The plan does not consider this prior art interaction before proposing a redundant/potentially-overbroad grant.
findings.location: AC: Capability — 'plus submit permission on `refs/heads/*`' + Files section: `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh`

findings.finding: The All-Projects refs/meta/config capability push (setup-all-projects.sh) has no explicit rollback or back-out plan. Modifying global Gerrit capabilities affects every project on the instance; if the grant needs to be reverted there is no described mechanism to do so.
findings.location: AC#2 (Capability) / infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' section to the plan (and a comment block in setup-all-projects.sh) that describes: (1) clone All-Projects, (2) remove the [capability] block from project.config, (3) push back to refs/meta/config. Alternatively add a teardown script that inverts the grant.

findings.finding: setup-all-projects.sh's convergence model for All-Projects refs/meta/config is unspecified: the plan does not state whether the script performs a declarative overwrite (like setup-project.sh does for the rebar project) or a surgical/additive edit. If surgical, a re-run after an out-of-band capability revocation will be a no-op (idempotent but NOT convergent), leaving the system in a bad state silently.
findings.location: Files section / AC: Capability — infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh (NEW)
findings.suggested_fix: Follow the setup-project.sh pattern: the script should fetch refs/meta/config of All-Projects, overwrite only the [capability] section (or the entire file if the project owns it declaratively), compute a diff, and push only if changes are needed — making it both idempotent AND convergent. Add a DRY_RUN=1 mode consistent with setup-project.sh.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
